zh.lifeSummary: 當Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus於1174年出生在蘇格蘭Forfarshire福爾法爾時，她的父親Gille Crist 4th Earl of Angus20歲，母親Marjory of Scotland22歲。她於1200年與Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland在蘇格蘭雷福福爾佩斯利佩斯利 艾比結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1270年在她的家鄉享年96歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭雷福福爾佩斯利佩斯利 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus于1174年出生在苏格兰Forfarshire福爾法爾时，她的父亲Gille Crist 4th Earl of Angus20岁，母亲Marjory of Scotland22岁。她于1200年与Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland在苏格兰雷福福爾佩斯利佩斯利 艾比结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1270年在她的家乡享年96岁，埋葬在苏格兰雷福福爾佩斯利佩斯利 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: Gille
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gisele, Pierre.
French: variant of   Gilles  .
German: variant of   Gill  .

variants.name: Gilles
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Jacques, Pierre, Amie, Arsene, Berthony, Ghislaine, Gilles, Jean Marie, Jean-Baptiste, Maryse, Maxime, Odette.

French, Walloon, and Haitian: from the French personal name Gilles of which the original form was Latin Aegidius. This was the name of a 7th-century Provençal hermit, whose cult popularized the name in a variety of more or less mutilated forms: Gidi and Gidy in southern France, Gil(l)i in the area of the Alpes-Maritimes, and Gil(l)e elsewhere. This last form was taken over to England by the Normans (see Giles 1).
German: from the personal name Gilius, from Aegilius, a Late Latin variant of Aegidius (see Giles 1).

nameMeanings.name: Christ
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Fritz, Kurt, Erwin, Franz, Georg, Gerhard, Heinz, Inge, Klaus, Liselotte, Udo.

German and Dutch: from a short form of the personal name Christian , or a nickname meaning ‘the Christian’. In both cases it is (ultimately) from the Latin name Christus ‘Christ’, which is from Greek Christos, a derivative of chriein ‘to anoint’, a calque of Hebrew mashiaḥ ‘Messiah’, which likewise means literally ‘the anointed’. This is the title given to Jesus of Nazareth (see Jesus ), as embodying the fulfilment of Messianic prophecy and expectation; he is the central figure of Christianity and most Christians believe he is the incarnation of God the Son. Compare Chrest , Crist , Ghrist , and Krist .
English: variant of   Crist  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Christopher
meanings: English, German, West Indian (mainly Trinidad and Tobago, Antigua and Barbuda, and British Virgin Islands), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the English and German personal name Christopher, from Greek Christophoros ‘Christ-bearing’ (see Christ 1). This was borne by a rather obscure 3rd-century Christian martyr. His name was relatively common among early Christians, who desired to bear Jesus Christ metaphorically with them in their daily lives. Subsequently, the name was explained by a folk etymology according to which the saint carried the infant Christ across a ford and so became the patron saint of travelers. Despite the widespread veneration and depiction of this saint, this was not a very common personal name in medieval England, and may in some instances have a habitational origin, for someone living for example in Saint Christopher parish (Saint Christopher le Stocks, London). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed the German variant Christoffer and cognates from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Kristóf and Czech, Slovak, Slovenian, and Croatian Krištof (see Kristof ). The usual German form of the name is Christoph .

variants.name: Jesus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Angel, Fernando, Gilberto, Juan, Lopez, Mario, Adelina, Adelino, Alicia, Alonzo.  Portuguese Catarina, Joao, Joaquim, Paulo.

Spanish (Jesús) and Portuguese: either from a personal name taken in honor of Jesus Christ, in Christianity the divine son of God the Father (see Christ 1), or from a short form of any compound name composed of any personal name + de Jesús. The name Jesus is from the Greek form, Iēsous, of Aramaic Yeshua, from Hebrew Yoshua, a byform of Yehoshuah (English Joshua) ‘God is salvation’.

variants.name: Krist
meanings: German and Dutch: variant of   Christ  .
North German: from a short form of the personal name   Kristen   or one of its variants.

Czech, Slovak, and Slovenian: from the German personal name Krist, an old variant of Christ , or from a short form of the personal name Czech and Slovak Kristián (see Christian ), Slovenian Kristan . This surname is very rare in Slovenia.

variants.name: Christian
meanings: German and French: from the personal name Christian, from Latin Christianus ‘follower of Christ’ (see Christ 1), literally ‘the Christian’. The usual French form is, however, Chrétien (see Chretien ). For the cognate English name see 3 below.
Manx: from Mac Kristinn ‘son of Kristinn’, a borrowing of the Old Norse form of Latin Christianus ‘the Christian’. Christian is a learned, Anglicized form.

English (of Norman origin): from the interchangeable Middle English personal names Cristian and Cristin, used for both men and women. Cristian is from Latin Christianus (see 1 above) and its female equivalent Christiana. Cristin is from Latin Christinus and Christina, male and female diminutives of Christus ‘Christ’. They were introduced to England and Scotland by the Normans in their Old French forms, male Crestien (or the learned form Cristian) and Cristin, female Cristiane and Cristine. Cristin(e) was naturally associated with the Middle English word cristen, cristin, or criston ‘Christian’ (Old English crīsten), reinforcing the tendency to use Cristin and Cristian as alternative name forms. The male name was never common in medieval England, but the female name became increasingly popular in the 14th and 15th centuries.

variants.name: Cristo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Adolfo, Carlos, Eduardo, Emilio, Guadalupe, Jorge, Juan, Juan Ramon, Livia, Manuel, Rebeca, Rey.  Italian Carlo, Dino, Marcello, Sal.
Spanish, Italian (Sardinia and Lazio), and Portuguese: from the personal name Cristo ‘Christ’, from Latin Christus, Greek Christos (see Christ 1).
American shortened and altered form of any of various Greek derivatives of the personal name   Christos  .

variants.name: Chrest
meanings: Americanized form of German   Christ  .

variants.name: Chryst
meanings: Americanized form of German   Christ  .

variants.name: Crist
meanings: English: apparently a nickname from Middle English Crist, Old English Crīst ‘Christ’ (see Christ ), possibly applied as a nickname for someone who played the part of Christ in a pageant.
English: from a pet form of the personal names   Christian   or   Christopher  .
Americanized form of German, Dutch, Czech, or Slovak   Krist   and German or Dutch   Christ  .

variants.name: Ghrist
meanings: Americanized form of German   Christ  .
History: The surname Ghrist was first recorded in the late 1700s in PA.

variants.name: Chriss
meanings: English (Sussex): variant of   Christ   (see   Crist  ). This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

American shortened form of any of various Greek derivatives of the personal name   Christos  , and possibly also of some related or similarly pronounced Slavic surnames (see   Kris  ). Compare   Chris   and   Criss  .

nameMeanings.name: Beatrix
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Late Latin personal name, which was borne by a saint martyred in Rome, together with Saints Faustinus and Simplicius, in the early 4th century. The original form of the name was probably Viatrix, a feminine version of Viator ‘voyager (through life)’, which was a favourite name among early Christians. This was then altered by association with Latin Beatus ‘blessed’ (Via- and Bea- sometimes being pronounced the same in Late Latin). See also Beatrice .

en.lifeSummary: When Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus was born in 1174, in Forfar, Forfarshire, Scotland, her father, Gille Crist 4th Earl of Angus, was 20 and her mother, Marjory of Scotland, was 22. She married Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland in 1200, in Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. She died in 1270, in her hometown, at the age of 96, and was buried in Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus est née en 1174, à Forfar, Forfarshire, Écosse, son père, Gille Crist 4th Earl of Angus, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Marjory of Scotland, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland en 1200, à Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée en 1270, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 96 ans, et a été enterrée à Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus 1174 in Forfar, Forfarshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Gille Crist 4th Earl of Angus 20 und ihre Mutter Marjory of Scotland 22. Sie hat 1200 in Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb 1270 im Alter von 96 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus è nata il nel 1174 nel Forfar, Forfarshire, Scotland, sua padre, Gille Crist 4th Earl of Angus, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Marjory of Scotland, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland nel 1200, nel Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 5 figlie. Morì nel 1270, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 96 anni, e fu sepolta a Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angusが1174年にスコットランドForfarshireフォーファルで生まれた時，父Gille Crist 4th Earl of Angusは20歳，母Marjory of Scotlandは22歳であった。1200年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアペイスリーペイスリー アビーでWalter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotlandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1270年に故郷で96歳で死去し，スコットランドレンフリューシュアイアペイスリーペイスリー アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus(이)가 1174년 스컷랜드 Forfarshire 포파르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gille Crist 4th Earl of Angus(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marjory of Scotland(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1200년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 파이슬리 파이슬리 애브브이에서 Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1270년 그녀의 고향에서 96세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 파이슬리 파이슬리 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus nasceu em 1174, em Forfar, Forfarshire, Scotland, seu pai, Gille Crist 4th Earl of Angus, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Marjory of Scotland, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland em 1200, em Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1270, em sua cidade natal, com 96 anos, e foi sepultada em Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus родилась в 1174 г. в местности форфар, Forfarshire, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Gille Crist 4th Earl of Angus было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Marjory of Scotland, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland в 1200 г. в местности пеисли эбби, пеисли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1270 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 96 лет и была похоронена в местности пеисли эбби, пеисли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Beatrix Bethoc Gille Christ de angus nació en 1174, en Forfar, Forfarshire, Scotland, su papá, Gille Crist 4th Earl of Angus, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Marjory of Scotland, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Walter Stewart 3rd High Steward of Scotland en 1200, en Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en 1270, en su pueblo, a la edad de 96 años, y fue sepultada en Paisley Abbey, Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.
